strengths: Consistent use of the Communication ATL skill, evidenced by proactive emails to advisors and peers and coordination across Slack, Discord and email
Creative application of Thinking ATL skill when you shifted from Python to block coding to optimize robot control
Strong Self-Management practices: you designed a structured workflow, maintained focus under pressure, and used a daily alarm to combat procrastination
Detailed Scratch program screenshot that illustrates sustained critical thinking and iterative problem-solving
weaknesses: Descriptions of Thinking and Communication skills lack concrete examples in some sections; adding one or two specific instances will show their direct impact
Follow-up experiments after supervisor feedback on LEGO fragility are not documented; recording these trials would illustrate hands-on application of skills
Pros & Cons list is organized but omits source citations; specifying where each point was sourced would strengthen evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You demonstrate effective application of ATL skills, especially communication and self-management, through clear examples of outreach and proactive time-management strategies. Your critical thinking is well-illustrated by the coding pivot and detailed program artifacts. To deepen this section, ensure every ATL skill you name is paired with a concrete, detailed example and document the iterations you performed in response to feedback. Citing the origin of each research finding will further validate the rigor of your approach.

strengths: Thorough brainstorming demonstrated by a detailed mind map that explores a wide range of personal project ideas
Well-articulated rationale for selecting LEGO robotics, showing clear alignment between personal interests and the chosen product
Comprehensive monthly timeline that sequences research, design and testing phases, demonstrating feasibility and resource planning
Clearly defined Aesthetics success criterion with a specific 70% satisfaction metric and evaluation method
Impact criterion articulated with precise user-confirmation measures to assess real-world effectiveness
weaknesses: Learning goal lacks precise, measurable success criteria (e.g. number of coding challenges, target dates) to make progress assessable
Size criterion is incomplete without a defined weight capacity or testing methodology
Intended audience metric is too vague and needs a structured feedback mechanism (e.g. survey with rating scale)
Research sources are cited in overly broad terms (YouTube, websites) rather than specific channels, articles or experts
Connection narrative contains engaging personal anecdotes that distract from planning; these should be summarized and focused on their relevance to the project plan
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates strong initial exploration and a clear project rationale, particularly in uniting your interests through LEGO robotics. The detailed timeline and precise aesthetics and impact criteria ensure that many success factors are measurable and user-centered. To strengthen your plan further, refine your learning goal and all criteria so that each is tied to a specific, quantifiable target and testing method. Additionally, sharpen your evidence by citing concrete sources and streamlining personal anecdotes to directly support your planning process.

strengths: Insightful reflection on improvements in time management and perseverance, clearly linking them back to your learning goal
Balanced evaluation of successes and challenges, including procrastination, and well-articulated plans for personal growth
Strong metacognitive insight into how you applied self-management, risk-taking, and critical thinking throughout the project
Forward-looking reflection that connects acquired technical and soft skills to future challenges and sets clear development priorities
weaknesses: Reflection does not evaluate each success criterion individually with supporting evidence; adding a criterion-by-criterion analysis would deepen your evaluation
While you identify acquired skills, specific examples of how you will apply these skills in future contexts are limited
Metacognitive observations could be enhanced by outlining concrete next steps or resources to build on identified strengths
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection section excels in personal insight, acknowledging both achievements and areas for improvement with clarity and depth. You connect your project experience to broader learning outcomes and future goals. To elevate this reflection, provide a structured evaluation against each success criterion with specific examples of evidence, and detail actionable next steps for applying newly acquired skills in upcoming projects.

annotations.comment: Your learning goal clearly identifies the product focus but lacks measurable success criteria. Add specific targets (e.g. complete X coding challenges by date Y) to make progress traceable and assessable.
Strong use of the Communication ATL skill: you proactively emailed your advisor and peers for feedback, demonstrating effective engagement.
You effectively expanded Communication ATL by coordinating meetings across Discord and email, showing adaptability in multiple media.
The connection narrative is richly detailed but could be more concise. Focus on two or three key experiences that directly illustrate how your interests support your learning goal.
The personal letter to your father is an engaging anecdote but distracts from planning. Consider summarizing this evidence to strengthen focus on how it informed your project plan.
Your mind map demonstrates broad and thorough brainstorming of personal project ideas. This range shows strong initial planning and exploration.
Clear rationale for selecting LEGO roboticsâcombining two interests into a single projectâshows focused decision-making aligned with your learning goal.
The Size criterion is incomplete. Define a specific weight capacity (e.g. âmust carry 5 kgâ) and describe exactly how you will test this to evaluate success.
The Intended Audience metric (âmake disabled users happyâ) is too vague. Specify a feedback method (e.g. survey rating â¥ X/5) and a timeline for gathering responses.
Your Aesthetics criterion includes a clear 70 % satisfaction metric and a defined evaluation method. This makes your success measureable and user-centered.
The Impact criterion is well-defined with a user-confirmation measure. This clarity ensures you can evaluate whether the product truly eases usersâ tasks.
Your plan outlines a detailed monthly timeline (research by Nov, design by Dec, testing by Jan). This sequencing demonstrates strong feasibility and resource allocation.
Research sources are listed too broadly (e.g. âYouTube, websitesâ). Cite specific channels, articles or experts to strengthen the credibility of your plan.
You identify Thinking and Communication as key ATL skills but provide no concrete examples here. Add a specific instance where these skills directly advanced your learning.
Excellent example of the Thinking ATL skill: switching from Python to block coding to optimize control shows creative critical thinking.
You note supervisor feedback on LEGO fragility but donât document the follow-up experiments you conducted. Record specific tests or material trials to show skill application.
Your Pros & Cons list shows structured research, but lacks citation of sources or methods. Specify where each pro/con was sourced to strengthen evidence.
Strong Self-Management example: you created a structured workflow, solved issues under pressure, and maintained focus throughout complex tasks.
Strong personal reflection on improvements in time management and perseverance links your growth back to the learning goal effectively.
Innovative use of a daily alarm demonstrates effective Self-Management to combat procrastination and keep your project on schedule.
The detailed Scratch program screenshot evidences sustained Thinking ATL through complex coding structures and persistent iteration.
Insightful reflection on how Self-Management balanced your academic, athletic, and project commitments shows strong personal evaluation.
Your reflection identifies key learnings but stops short of evaluating each success criterion with evidence. Add criterion-by-criterion analysis for depth.
Excellent evaluative insight connecting project outcomes with your learning goal and setting priorities for future coding focus.
Thorough self-reflection acknowledges both achievements and procrastination challenges, and outlines clear areas for personal improvement.
Effective metacognitive reflection highlights perseverance, time management, and risk-taking as key learnings about yourself.
Strong reflection on improved communication, creativity, and critical thinking shows awareness of personal development through this project.
Clear identification of acquired technical and soft skills demonstrates deep reflection; consider adding examples of how youâll apply them next.
Balanced evaluation of positive and negative ATL skill development demonstrates nuanced understanding; use this insight to refine your planning.
Good forward-looking reflection linking project skills to future challenges; consider outlining specific next steps to build on these skills.
